summary: Pediatric housestaff do not influence test orderings in the NICU because diagnostic decisions result from interdisciplinary teamwork led by specialists such as neonatologists, nurses, and technologists—without any documentation supporting their initiative or impact on testing choices. The available literature consistently shows joint evaluation processes and protocol-driven screening practices instead of primary ownership by junior medical personnel.
